input.prompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

data.content:
Use an MSBuild condition that matches your test-project naming convention. In your `Directory.Build.props`:

```xml
<Project>
  <!-- Properties for all projects -->
  <PropertyGroup>
    <!-- ... -->
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- Only for test projects -->
  <PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

**Key points:**

- `$(MSBuildProjectName)` is the project filename without the extension, available before any targets run, so it's safe in a `Directory.Build.props`.
- Adjust the condition if your convention differs (e.g., `.Test`, `.IntegrationTests`). You can broaden it with `Contains('Test')` but that risks false positives.
- `EnableMSTestRunner` opts into the MTP runner; `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` enables `dotnet test` compatibility with MTP.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about how to conditionally set MSBuild properties in a `Directory.Build.props` file so they only apply to test projects (not production/library projects) when migrating from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP).

This is a knowledge question — no codebase exploration needed.

The standard approach is to use an MSBuild condition that checks for the presence of the MSTest runner package reference, or more commonly, to condition on the project file name convention (e.g., `*.Tests.csproj`), or to use the `IsTestProject` property.

The cleanest approach for MTP migration is to condition on `$(IsTestProject)` which MSTest SDK sets, or to match on project naming convention.


Now I'll provide a straightforward, practical solution.
